name: override_detection_and_confirmation
description: Warm day, HVAC in cool mode at comfort_cool (74F). User bumps thermostat to heat mode at 14:00. After 10-minute confirm period passes (14:10), override is confirmed. Classification cycle at 14:30 is suppressed by active override.
unsupported_reason: Integration-tier: override detection and confirmation lifecycle is driven by the coordinator's _async_thermostat_changed state listener and an async_call_later confirm timer. The Tier-A harness dispatches thermostat_state_changed events but the confirm-delay timer that promotes an override_detected to override_confirmed is a coordinator-layer async_call_later callback — not wired in the headless harness. Additionally, post-#249 the engine sets dual-temp (comfort_heat/comfort_cool) rather than single-side 74F, so expect_temp=74.0 assertions are stale. The final assertion (no_action after override clears) also fails because the engine emits 'resumed' after override_cleared rather than suppressing the classification. Full lifecycle requires coordinator state-listener + timer layer. Deferred to Tier-B HeadlessTarry harness (#236 follow-on).
notes: Tests the full override detection → confirmation → classification suppression lifecycle.
Gap 3 (override confirmation delay) is NOT yet in simulate.py — override_detected and override_confirmed assertions are marked track:integration.
What the simulator CAN test today: classification with manual_override_active=True produces no_action (not classification_applied).
The manual_override_active flag must be set via config initial state for the logic-track assertion to work.
Once Gap 3 is closed in simulate.py, the integration assertions can be promoted to logic-track.

events.note: Warm summer afternoon. Indoor at comfort_cool=74F. HVAC holding cool mode.
Warm-day classification: HVAC cool at comfort_cool=74F.
User bumps thermostat to heat mode. Override detection fires. 10-minute confirm window starts.
10 minutes have elapsed. Thermostat still in heat mode → override confirmed.
Scheduled 30-min classification cycle. Override is active — should be suppressed.

assertions.reason: Initial warm-day classification applied: hvac_mode=cool, target=comfort_cool=74F.
Thermostat state changed to heat mode while classification says cool — override detection fires.
assertions.note: Override detection fires in coordinator _async_thermostat_changed listener.

assertions.reason: 10 minutes elapsed with thermostat still in heat mode → override_confirm_seconds=600 expired → override confirmed.
assertions.note: Confirm timer is an async_call_later callback in the coordinator layer.

assertions.reason: Classification cycle while manual_override_active=True produces no_action (override suppresses classification).

verdict.summary: Override detected on thermostat mode change, confirmed after 10-min delay, suppresses subsequent classification
verdict.observed_behavior: classification_applied (cool, 74F) → override_detected (14:00) → override_confirmed (14:10) → no_action on classification (14:30)
verdict.expected_behavior: CA respects user's manual override and stops re-applying classification until override clears
